content:
{
  "title": "Semantic Sketch Prompting: Leveraging High-Level Intent for Improved Code Generation",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for code generation often struggle to capture the programmer’s high-level intent, leading to syntactically correct but semantically inadequate code. Existing approaches typically rely on plain natural language descriptions or code snippets as context.",
  "motivation": "Bridging the gap between human intent and code output is critical for deploying LLMs in real-world software development. As models grow in capability, richer prompts can unlock more powerful and accurate code synthesis. Incorporating structured semantic cues into prompts could ground model outputs to desired functionalities and reduce hallucinations.",
  "method": "We propose Semantic Sketch Prompting, where a prompt combines (1) a natural language task description, (2) a high-level pseudo-code outline specifying function signatures and control flow, and (3) optional semantic type annotations. The method automatically generates these sketches from user input or allows users to provide them directly. The LLM receives a concatenated prompt: description, sketch, and constraints, guiding generation towards both syntactic correctness and semantic fidelity. Ablation studies will investigate the contribution of each sketch component.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on HumanEval, MBPP, and APPS datasets, comparing against standard natural language prompts and code infilling baselines. Metrics include pass@k, functional correctness, and semantic similarity to reference implementations. Human evaluation will assess code readability and intent alignment."
}
